name: ApplyMixed_Cold
desc: measures the cold mutation path: mixed-step config against a fresh target each iteration so Execute always runs. Complement to the converged _NoOp variants.

name: ApplyNoOp
desc: measures the converged-state Apply path for posix.copy steps. Drift detection runs; Execute is skipped because the target already has the desired content.

name: ApplyNoOp_Container
desc: is the container.instance variant — drift detection on containers already running with the desired image / ports / env.

name: ApplyNoOp_Dir
desc: is the directory-step variant — drift detection on pre-existing dirs with matching mode/owner.

name: ApplyNoOp_Firewall
desc: is the firewall-step variant — drift detection on firewall rules already in place.

name: ApplyNoOp_Group
desc: is the group-step variant — drift detection on groups already present with the desired GID/members.

name: ApplyNoOp_Mixed
desc: exercises a configuration with multiple step kinds at once (copy, dir, symlink, template). Heavier per- action work than the uniform _NoOp variants; catches scheduler / DAG regressions that single-kind benches miss.

name: ApplyNoOp_Mount
desc: is the mount-step variant — drift detection on mounts already present in /etc/fstab and mounted. Capped at Size-1000 because findFstabEntry is O(N) per step → O(N^2) total.

name: ApplyNoOp_Pkg
desc: is the package-step variant — drift detection on packages already installed via the MemTarget backend.

name: ApplyNoOp_REST
desc: is the rest.request variant — GET-driven drift detection against MemREST. Reports http/op alongside ns/op since REST resources don't route through MemTarget.Commands.

name: ApplyNoOp_Run
desc: is the run-step variant — idempotency check returns success so the apply command is skipped.

name: ApplyNoOp_Runset
desc: is the run_set-step variant — set-diff logic where the list command already returns the desired items.

name: ApplyNoOp_Service
desc: is the service-step variant — drift detection on services already in the desired running/enabled state.

name: ApplyNoOp_Symlink
desc: is the symlink-step variant of BenchmarkApplyNoOp — drift detection on pre-existing symlinks.

name: ApplyNoOp_Sysctl
desc: is the sysctl-step variant — drift detection issues one `sysctl -n` per step, so cmds/op should scale linearly with step count.

name: ApplyNoOp_Template
desc: is the template-step variant — drift detection on rendered template output that already matches.

name: ApplyNoOp_Unarchive_Tar
desc: is the uncompressed-tar unarchive variant — same drift-detect shape as the .tar.gz bench.

name: ApplyNoOp_Unarchive_TarGz
desc: is the .tar.gz variant of the unarchive bench. Drift detection via the cached content hash; no re-extraction.

name: ApplyNoOp_Unarchive_TarXz
desc: is the xz-compressed unarchive variant — same drift-detect shape as the .tar.gz bench.

name: ApplyNoOp_Unarchive_TarZst
desc: is the zstd-compressed unarchive variant — same drift-detect shape as the .tar.gz bench.

name: ApplyNoOp_Unarchive_Zip
desc: is the .zip unarchive variant — same drift-detect shape as the .tar.gz bench.

name: ApplyNoOp_User
desc: is the user-step variant — drift detection on users already present with the desired shell/home/groups.

name: DiagnosticEmission
desc: measures the cost of raising one diagnostic through the emitter pipeline. Catches regressions in event routing and template rendering.

name: LoadConfig
desc: measures the full scampi load pipeline: lex, parse, resolve, evaluate. Tracks language-layer overhead per step count.
